why: A clear problem statement is the foundation everything else in your report gets built on.
Knowing who has this problem shapes the market and demand research in your report.

text: How would you make money from it?
why: How you'd make money from this shapes the financial modelling in your report.

text: How much money do you have to put into this?
subtext: Whatever getting started would take — equipment, tools, marketing. An exact figure or a best guess both work.
why: Your budget shapes what's realistic now versus later — and an exact figure makes the report's budget-fit verdict exact too. Enter it in your own currency; the report prices everything using costs for your country.
